spec_match.rationale: Concepts exercise the exact spec surfaces: the three-method session lifecycle with per-state button gating (logging-lifecycle), allowUpload+metadata with the ≤5-entry/≤100-char limits (bug-report-companion), and the full binding/policy gate (capability-policy-probe). Metadata-exception copy now matches BOTH authorities: WICG spec mandates TypeError, Chromium main rejects with RangeError (rtc.cc lines 35–42 via RTCMetadataValidator, rtc_logging_utils.h kMaxMetadataSize=5/kMaxMetadataLength=100 UTF-8 bytes) — presented as a labelled divergence, not an error.

interactive.rationale: Real controls drive real behavior: start/finish/cancel buttons enabled only in valid states; metadata row add/remove with live count preview; re-run probe. Unsupported browsers get honest disabled controls and flag instructions, never a faked session.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index cards now match what the concepts deliver, including the divergence note on the bug-report-companion card; the enabling-it section is provenanced to the direct chromestatus API (updated 2026-06-25: OT desktop 150–155, ship estimate 156, status 'Proposed').

edge_cases.rationale: Covers the honest negative branches: unbound navigator.rtc (disabled buttons + flag note), per-surface method absence (probe pills), over-limit metadata preview before the call, and the UTF-8-bytes-vs-JS-length nuance for non-ASCII metadata.

references.rationale: Feature index cites chromestatus.com/feature/5091582546149376, the WICG spec, the explainer, and w3c/webrtc-extensions#124; bug-report-companion's divergence note links the Chromium rtc.cc implementation directly. The blink-dev Intent to Experiment (msg16832) corroborates the OT window, ship estimate, and enterprise policy.

conformance.rationale: New immutable feature suite (this cycle, 7 assertions): navigator.rtc binding, the three method types (spec IDL names), the [SecureContext] gate, and both metadata-limit rejections — written to accept TypeError (spec) OR RangeError (current Chromium) so the suite asserts enforcement, not a transitory error name. Verified executing live at /v150/webrtc-diagnostic-logging-api/conformance/.

runtime_evidence.rationale: chrome-devtools-mcp on Chrome stable (2026-07-24): all visible controls exercised at 1280×800 and 390×844 — probe re-run (4 no-pills + honest status), lifecycle buttons disabled when unbound, metadata add/remove/count-note updates with over-limit warnings, zero console errors and zero failed requests across all four pages.

telemetry_health.rationale: Concepts emit showcaseTelemetry.assert events per branch (rtc-probe-consistent/ran, rtc-start/finish/cancel, rtc-metadata-limit-enforced — now accepting either enforced error name); telemetry POSTs returned 202 during validation.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables throughout, button background+color pairs, focus-visible styles, no static-card pattern; edits were copy/attribute-level only and preserve the existing layout.

accessibility.rationale: role=status/aria-live on status lines and call log, aria-labels on metadata inputs and remove buttons, semantic probe table with aria-label; this cycle added aria-live=polite to the metadata count-note so over-limit warnings are announced (forms guidance), and row labels keep inputs named.

mobile_desktop_parity.rationale: Matrix pass after two in-place fixes found by this cycle's own validation: the feature index overflowed at 390px via three unbreakable code tokens (navigator.rtc.startDiagnosticLogging(), WebRtcDiagnosticLogCollectionAllowedForOrigins ×2) — fixed with page-local code{overflow-wrap:anywhere} (now clean at both 390×844 and 360×740); the probe table overflowed at 390px (449px, nowrap method-name cells) — fixed with overflow-wrap:anywhere + dropped nowrap (now 342px). All four pages: scrollWidth equals viewport at 1280, 390, and 360 with every interactive state exercised; main action buttons measure 45px, metadata-row remove buttons 30px (above the 24px WCAG 2.2 AA floor).

openQuestions.title: Re-verify origin-trial registration and ship status when chromestatus next updates
openQuestions.detail: As of 2026-07-24 the chromestatus detail (updated 2026-06-25) records the OT window desktop 150–155 and ship estimate 156/'Proposed', but the OT stage carries no origin_trial_id — so the family copy deliberately avoids a generic 'register for the trial' claim. If an origin_trial_id appears (public registration opens), update the index enabling-it section and capability-policy-probe gate step 2 with the registration link.

guidanceConsulted.query: correcting technical copy and error names in demo pages without changing layout or interaction semantics
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Associate labels with inputs, use aria-live for dynamic error announcements, and never rely on placeholder-only naming.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied: the metadata count-note (a dynamic warning) gained aria-live=polite so over-limit announcements reach assistive tech; metadata key/value inputs already carry per-row aria-labels; no layout or interaction change was needed for the copy corrections.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: chrome-devtools-mcp a11y-tree + live count-note updates during metadata add/remove at 390×844

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region announcements for dynamic probe/status results after button activation
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce dynamic state through live regions tied to the activating control; keep semantic tables and named controls.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Verified applied across the family (status lines + call log live regions observed updating in the a11y tree; probe table aria-labelled; disabled states exposed via the disableable/disabled semantics).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: chrome-devtools-mcp a11y-tree snapshots on all three concepts, 2026-07-24

summary: Source-review hardening of the WebRTC Diagnostic Logging family: retained the RangeError metadata claim after producing direct Chromium source evidence (rtc.cc RejectWithRangeError ×2; rtc_logging_utils.h limits 5 entries / 100 UTF-8 bytes) and reframed it as a labelled spec(TypeError)-vs-implementation(RangeError) divergence; provenanced availability to the direct chromestatus API (updated 2026-06-25: OT desktop 150–155, estimate 156, 'Proposed'); replaced the generic origin-trial registration claim with the evidenced state (no origin_trial_id listed as of 2026-07-24, enterprise-oriented trial, WebRtcDiagnosticLogCollectionAllowedForOrigins policy verified in chromium pref_mapping + blink-dev intent msg16832); added the feature's missing immutable conformance suite (7 assertions) and aria-live on the metadata count-note; fixed two mobile-overflow defects found during validation (index code tokens, probe table). logging-lifecycle needed no copy change (verified accurate).
